_meta.description: Proportional Reporting Ratio (PRR) analysis of VAERS data
_meta.methodology: PRR = (reports of symptom X for vaccine V / all reports for V) / (reports of symptom X for all other vaccines / all reports for other vaccines). Signals: PRR ≥ 2, ≥ 3 cases, chi² ≥ 3.84 (p < 0.05).
_meta.interpretation: A PRR > 1 means a symptom is reported MORE frequently for a given vaccine compared to all other vaccines. This is a statistical SIGNAL, not proof of causation. Higher PRR = stronger signal.
limitations: VAERS is passive surveillance — reporting rates vary by vaccine and time period
COVID vaccines have massive reporting volume that skews comparisons
PRR does not account for dose denominators (number of people vaccinated)
Stimulated reporting during COVID pandemic inflates signals for non-COVID vaccines too
A signal is a hypothesis to investigate, NOT evidence of causation
